scope.summary: F-LOW DB3 verified already satisfied: a failed owned-stream recovery marks the slot's quarantine_reason durably, and every later access returns the same typed Quarantined/UnknownDiscarded outcome. This close records verification, not new work.
out_of_scope: Any code change. The misleading temporarily-unavailable path the finding describes no longer occurs after a recovery failure.
Pool-side quarantine (crates/oraclemcp-db/src/pool.rs), a separate slot lifecycle that this finding does not name and that is another agent's mid-edit.

entry_points.name: quarantine_connection_slot / replace_connection_slot / lock_inner
entry_points.trace: Both into_connection failure sites (connection.rs:2900 recovery, 5802 chunked fallback) route through quarantine_connection_slot, which sets quarantine_reason via get_or_insert (1353) so the first reason is durable. replace_connection_slot (2734) and lock_inner (1393) both check quarantine_reason first and return Quarantined{UnknownDiscarded}. The bead's negative acceptance is tested directly by owned_stream_recovery_failure_persists_quarantine_for_later_calls, which injects a recovery failure and asserts BOTH the first error and a subsequent lock_inner return the same typed Quarantined/UnknownDiscarded with the identical message, explicitly refusing temporary unavailability.

limitations: Verification of pre-existing state, not new implementation.

The positive half (successful recovery restores the connection exactly once and later queries stay usable) is covered by the surrounding recovery tests rather than by a dedicated assertion in the same test.
Scoped verification: cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --lib. No workspace-wide run.
